user_query:
I'm an Armenian pro long jumper coming back from a left hamstring strain. Please create a concise, medically grounded readiness check and communication package using my local data and reputable rehab guidance.

Inputs to read (all under input/):
- input/athlete_profile.yaml
- input/training_log.csv
- input/lab_results.csv
- input/doctor_notes.md
- input/email_draft.txt

What to produce:
1) Analysis report (JSON): outputs/report/hamstring_readiness_report.json
   Requirements:
   - Period analyzed: 2026-04-01 to 2026-04-14 (inclusive).
   - Compute weekly aggregates for each calendar week (Mon–Sun) fully or partially within the period:
     • For each week: total sprints_meters, total jumps_count, and mean RPE.
   - Flag training load spikes where weekly sprints_meters increase ≥30% vs the previous week.
   - Flag labs with these thresholds: CK ≥500 U/L; Ferritin <30 ng/mL; Vitamin D (25-OH) <30 ng/mL.
   - From input/doctor_notes.md, extract 2–5 key points specifically about return-to-sport criteria and sprint exposure limits.
   - Include a list of the guideline source filenames you used (from step 2 below).
   - JSON schema (keys must exist):
     {
       "athlete": {"name": string, "event": string},
       "period_analyzed": {"start": "YYYY-MM-DD", "end": "YYYY-MM-DD"},
       "weekly_load": [
         {"week_start": "YYYY-MM-DD", "week_end": "YYYY-MM-DD", "sprints_meters": number, "jumps_count": number, "mean_RPE": number}
       ],
       "flags": {"training": [string], "labs": [string], "doctor_note_key_points": [string]},
       "guideline_sources_used": [string]
     }

2) Download official guidance and extract snippets:
   - Find and download at least two publicly available guidance documents that address hamstring strain rehabilitation or return-to-sport criteria from reputable organizations (must be one of: an official sports medicine or sports governing body, or a government/public health site). Acceptable publisher domains include .org and .gov, or the official domain of a sports federation (e.g., World Athletics) — no commercial blogs.
   - Save each file under outputs/downloads/ as guideline_1.ext, guideline_2.ext (use an appropriate extension, e.g., .pdf or .html). If you download more than two, name guideline_3.ext, etc.
   - Create outputs/logs/sources.json capturing for each downloaded file: {"filename", "title" (best-effort), "publisher" (best-effort), "year" (best-effort), "url"}.
   - From each downloaded file, extract all sentences that contain "hamstring" and at least one of {"rehabilitation", "return", "criteria"} (case-insensitive). Save results to outputs/report/guideline_snippets.json with this structure: {"<filename>": {"snippets": [string], "no_matches": boolean}}. Set no_matches=true if none found.

3) Rewrite email for tone and brevity:
   - Using input/email_draft.txt and your findings, produce a professional, concise note to my team doctor.
   - Length: 120–180 words; 2–4 short paragraphs.
   - Clearly state: the analyzed period, any training or lab flags you found, and that you’ve attached hamstring_readiness_report.json and guideline_snippets.json for reference.
   - Save as outputs/communication/email_to_doctor.txt.

General notes:
- Do not include direct URLs in this prompt; locate appropriate sources yourself and record them in outputs/logs/sources.json.
- All outputs must be reproducible from the provided inputs plus the downloaded guidance files.
- If a step yields no matches (e.g., no guideline sentences), still produce the required files with the specified placeholders/flags so the workflow is complete. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
